findings.evidence: Grep shows scalar API at `Comodule/Hom.lean:75,91,134` and categorical `homModule` at `ComoduleCat.lean:103`, but composition API only has `add_comp`/`comp_add`/zero at `Comodule/Hom.lean:181,188,195,201` and `ComoduleCat.lean:261,268,275,282`; Mathlib’s `CategoryTheory.Linear` normal forms are `Linear.smul_comp`/`Linear.comp_smul`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` lemmas such as `smul_comp : comp (r • g) f = r • comp g f` and `comp_smul : comp g (r • f) = r • comp g f`, and mirror them for `ComoduleCat` morphisms.
findings.issue: `instModule` exposes `SMul R`/`Module R` on `Comodule.Hom`, but there are no characteristic lemmas for scalar multiplication through composition.

summary: The additive/nsmul/sum public API is now characterized, but the PR also exposes an `R`-module structure on comodule homs without the scalar-composition lemmas needed to use it abstractly. Downstream code still has to unfold to linear maps to prove composition is `R`-linear.
